today_summary: GCM26 (Jun '26, DTE 42) is trading $4,354 with ATM IV at 20.92% and a call-heavy OI structure of 110,783 calls vs 68,914 puts (P/C ratio 0.62). Intraday flow is decidedly bullish at 7,773 calls vs 3,378 puts (2.3:1 call/put vol). Max pain at $4,875 sits $521 above spot, maintaining structural upside magnetism with 42 days to expiry. Dealers are in near-neutral GEX territory (aggregate −7.07), consistent with a low-volatility drift environment — 1σ daily range is $57, implying a session band of roughly $4,297–$4,411.
five_day_thesis: The defining 5-day development is the near-complete GEX recovery from −213.52 (Jun 10) to −7.07 today — a normalization that parallels spot's +$239 bounce from the Jun 12 low of $4,115 and a 4.56pp IV compression from 25.48% to 20.92%, signaling a regime shift from vol-amplifying dealer posture back to stabilizing. Max pain marched lower continuously from $5,000 → $4,875 (−$125 over 5 sessions), narrowing the gap but preserving $521 of structural upside pull into June expiry. The $4,000 put wall has been the unshakeable bedrock for all five sessions — OI now stands at 4,935 contracts today vs 3,377 on Jun 10 (+46%), confirming that institutional protective puts are actively being rolled and expanded, not expiring. Call wall structure remained anchored at $5,350–$5,370 (8,010 contracts), providing a distant but fixed ceiling that leaves substantial runway above current prices. The combination of GEX normalization, IV compression, rising call OI, and persistent put floor anchoring positions the June contract for continued measured upside, with dips likely absorbed by the mechanical floor at $4,000.
what_changed: GEX aggregate recovered from −213.52 to −7.07 over 5 sessions — regime flip from vol-amplifying to near-neutral dealer posture
ATM IV compressed from 23.34% to 20.92% (−2.42pp) — structural shift favoring premium sellers and reducing realized vol
Max pain drifted lower from $5,000 to $4,875 (−$125) on a steady daily descent, narrowing the upside magnetic gap
Put wall at $4,000 locked for all 5 sessions and OI expanded from 3,377 to 4,935 (+46%) — active institutional floor-building
Spot recovered from $4,115 (Jun 12 low) to $4,354 (+$239), repairing the post-selloff technical damage
Today's call vol at 2.3× put vol (7,773 vs 3,378) — strongest intraday bullish flow of the 5-day window
prediction: Bias bullish above $4,300, where cumulative near-spot GEX turns call-dominant (+4.62 at $4,300) and dealer hedging flows become structurally supportive; the $4,355 strike adds the strongest positive GEX in the ATM zone (+7.27), creating a local gamma pin near current price. Path of least resistance targets $4,400 (double-sided OI wall: 1,865 calls + 1,994 puts) then $4,450 and $4,700 if momentum sustains; GEX gamma flip at $3,485 remains safely below, keeping the vol-damping regime intact. A sustained break below $4,300 reactivates the negative GEX cascade and reopens risk toward the $4,150 put wall (2,400 contracts).

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot +$67.5 over 5 days, GEX near-neutral, call vol 2.3× today, max pain $521 above. Bias flips bearish on a confirmed break below $4,300.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips to near-ATM zone — $4,350 strike holds 1,276 call OI + 1,250 put OI (balanced, liquid) and net GEX is +0.36 with the strongest call gamma in the region just above at $4,355 (net +7.27).
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Key GEX anchor (net +4.62, call dominant). Above = dealer long-gamma pinning regime, buy-the-dip dynamic. Below = negative GEX cascade reactivates, path to $4,150 opens.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Below the $4,280 GEX support cluster (net −1.58 at $4,280); −$55 (~1.3%) from entry midpoint. Below this negative GEX dominates to $4,150.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 $4,400: double-sided OI wall (1,865 call + 1,994 put) — take 60% off. T2 $4,450: call OI cluster (call_gex 10.85, net −5.45 = GEX headwind, full exit).
day_trade_cta.risk_note: IV 20.92%, GEX near-neutral (−7.07) → expect ~$57 daily 1σ range. Standard position size appropriate.
